target.title: [Bug]: ollama-cloud runtime fails DNS lookup for ai.ollama.com, while ollama/<model>:cloud works
target.body:
## Summary

- Add an Ollama doctor contract that flags and migrates retired `models.providers.ollama-cloud.baseUrl` / `baseURL` values pointing at `https://ai.ollama.com` to the canonical `https://ollama.com` endpoint.
- Include configured `models.providers` ids in plugin doctor compatibility discovery, so provider-scoped config such as `ollama-cloud` can invoke the owning plugin's doctor normalizer.
- Keep Ollama runtime URL resolution unchanged; legacy config is repaired through `openclaw doctor --fix` instead of silently overriding runtime configuration.

## Linked context

- Fixes #92391.
- Fix classification: root-cause compatibility migration fix.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High; the patch adds extension-specific contract tests for the Ollama doctor normalizer and registry tests for provider-id doctor discovery.
- Root cause: The doctor compatibility discovery path collected channel ids and plugin entry ids, but not `models.providers` ids, so a saved `models.providers.ollama-cloud.baseUrl` value with the retired `https://ai.ollama.com` host could remain in canonical config and continue to feed the Ollama runtime resolver.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The patch repairs the source of the bad runtime URL by making `ollama-cloud` provider config eligible for plugin doctor migrations, then the Ollama plugin normalizer rewrites the retired endpoint to the canonical endpoint before runtime uses the persisted provider base URL.
- What did NOT change: This only changes doctor compatibility migration and provider-id doctor discovery; it does not change Ollama chat streaming, model discovery, auth handling, default local Ollama endpoints, or unrelated provider behavior.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: A legacy `ollama-cloud` provider config with `https://ai.ollama.com` is now repaired by `openclaw doctor --fix` through the Ollama plugin doctor contract, preventing that retired host from staying in the provider base URL used by runtime config.
- Real setup used: Extension-specific contract tests and doctor registry unit tests in the OpenClaw test harness.
- Exact post-patch steps or command: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/ollama/doctor-contract-api.test.ts extensions/ollama/index.test.ts extensions/ollama/src/provider-base-url.test.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts extensions/ollama/provider-discovery.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (copied terminal output):

```text
Exit code: 0
RUN  v4.1.7 [redacted-local-path]

Test Files  5 passed (5)
Tests  88 passed (88)
```

- Observed outcome: The Ollama contract test verifies `https://ai.ollama.com` and `baseURL` aliases migrate to `https://ollama.com`, while the existing Ollama provider and stream tests remain green.
- What was not tested: External live proof against Ollama Cloud DNS or account credentials is not available in this local validation environment; completed local validation covers the config migration and runtime-adjacent provider behavior.

## Tests and validation

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/doctor-contract-registry.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-legacy-config-migrate.test.ts`
- Observed result after fix: The provider-id doctor discovery tests and compatibility migration bridge tests passed.
- Extension-specific validation: Ollama plugin contract test plus provider registry contract test confirm the plugin doctor path is reachable for `ollama-cloud` and the retired endpoint is normalized before runtime config use.

```text
Exit code: 0
RUN  v4.1.7 [redacted-local-path]

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)

RUN  v4.1.7 [redacted-local-path]

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  12 passed (12)
```

## Risk checklist

- Scope boundary: Only doctor compatibility migration and plugin doctor id discovery are changed.
- Runtime behavior: Ollama runtime URL selection remains unchanged after canonical config is repaired.
- Backward compatibility: Existing canonical `https://ollama.com` and local Ollama provider configs are left unchanged.

## Current review state

- Ready for maintainer review after local-submit-ready passes.
- No remote push, PR creation, or PR update has been performed yet.
